sections.title: PURPOSE AND SCOPE
sections.llm_prompt: Write Purpose and Scope for a Process Performance Qualification (PPQ) on {process_type} for {product_name}. This is Stage 2 of the FDA Lifecycle Approach to Process Validation. State the manufacturing process, facility, product strength(s), number of PPQ batches (typically 3 consecutive), and that this protocol demonstrates the commercial process is capable of consistently delivering quality product. {description}

sections.title: PROCESS DESCRIPTION
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the manufacturing process for {product_name}. Summarize each unit operation, equipment, materials, batch size, cycle time, and any critical process parameters (CPPs) and critical quality attributes (CQAs) established during Stage 1 (process design).

items.text: Analytical methods validated per ICH Q2(R2)
Cleaning validation complete for shared equipment
Personnel trained and qualified on procedures
Batch records approved and in effect
Risk assessment (ICH Q9) complete and residual risks acceptable

sections.title: CRITICAL PROCESS PARAMETERS AND CRITICAL QUALITY ATTRIBUTES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a table of Critical Process Parameters (CPPs) and Critical Quality Attributes (CQAs) for {product_name}. Columns: Parameter / Attribute | Type (CPP/CQA) | Target | Range | Justification | Measurement Frequency.

sections.title: SAMPLING AND TESTING PLAN
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a sampling and testing plan for PPQ of {product_name}. Sample at each critical process step including raw material, in-process (multiple points), bulk, final product, and environment/utility. Include enhanced sampling appropriate for PPQ (higher than routine). For each sample list: number of samples, location, test methods, acceptance criteria.

sections.title: ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
sections.llm_prompt: State the overall acceptance criteria for PPQ success. Include: (1) all CPPs maintained within proven acceptable range across all 3 batches, (2) all CQAs meet release specifications with appropriate statistical confidence, (3) intra-batch and inter-batch variability acceptable (e.g., RSD limits), (4) no unexplained deviations, (5) yield within target, (6) all in-process controls pass.

sections.title: STATISTICAL APPROACH
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the statistical approach for PPQ data analysis. Include: descriptive statistics for each CPP and CQA, capability indices (Cp, Cpk) where applicable, confidence intervals, trend analysis across batches. Reference FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011) and ICH Q8/Q9/Q10.

sections.title: DEVIATION AND EXCURSION HANDLING
sections.llm_prompt: Describe how deviations and CPP/CQA excursions during PPQ will be handled. Reference the deviation SOP, impact assessment on PPQ acceptance, investigation requirements, CAPA, and QA decision authority.

sections.title: CONTINUED PROCESS VERIFICATION (STAGE 3)
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the Continued Process Verification (CPV) plan for Stage 3 of process validation. Include: ongoing monitoring of CPPs and CQAs, trending (SPC control charts), periodic product review, annual product review (APR), and criteria for revalidation.

sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate references for a process validation protocol. Include: FDA Guidance for Industry Process Validation: General Principles and Practices (2011), EU GMP Annex 15, ICH Q7, Q8(R2), Q9, Q10, Q11, 21 CFR 211, PDA Technical Report 60, relevant company SOPs.

metadata.regulatory_framework: FDA Process Validation Guidance 2011, EU GMP Annex 15, ICH Q8-Q11, 21 CFR 211
